doc.title: The statements are a relay — IS → RE → BS
doc.event: Year-end totals for Maya's studio: revenues $48,000, expenses $31,000, beginning owner's capital $10,000, owner draws $8,000, assets $27,000, liabilities $8,000. Build the three statements and prove they connect.

variants.label: Each statement hands its answer to the next

computationPaths.narration: INCOME STATEMENT — a period's performance: revenues earned minus expenses incurred. Its bottom line is the baton.
STATEMENT OF OWNER'S EQUITY — how the owner's claim moved: contributions and income up, draws down.
BALANCE SHEET — a snapshot at the last instant of the period: A = L + E, with E being the baton just handed over. If it doesn't balance, the relay dropped somewhere upstream.

memorize.body: The relay: Net income (IS) → Statement of Owner's Equity → Ending capital → Balance Sheet's equity. Prepare them in that order; each needs the previous one's answer.
IS = a video of the period. BS = a photo of the last day. Equity's statement is the editing room where the two meet.
Draws (and dividends) are NOT expenses and never appear on the income statement — they're distributions of equity, living only on the equity statement.

questions.prompt: Revenues 48,000, expenses 31,000, beginning capital 10,000, draws 8,000. Ending owner's capital =

distractors.feedback: 27,000 forgets the draws — the owner took 8,000 out. 10,000 + 17,000 − 8,000 = 19,000.
9,000 treats draws as an expense inside net income AND subtracts them again — draws bypass the income statement entirely and subtract once, on the equity statement: 10,000 + 17,000 − 8,000 = 19,000.
